intelligence.hypothesis: Insufficient telemetry; awaiting next valid capture.

vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The registry is largely inconsistent with the visual evidence. P1 (String of Nickels) is absent/unidentifiable. P2/P4 (Mexican Mint/Silver Guest) are not visible as distinct specimens. P3 (Pothos) is the only identifiable biological presence, though it is severely compromised.

inventory_reconciliation.P1: Systemic Loss: No plant matter detected in yellow pot.
inventory_reconciliation.P2: Systemic Loss: No plant matter detected.
inventory_reconciliation.P3: Present: Pothos remains, though in a state of severe decline.
inventory_reconciliation.P4: Systemic Loss: No plant matter detected.

plant_audit.P1: Empty soil surface; no signs of life.
plant_audit.P2: Empty soil surface; no signs of life.
plant_audit.P3: Pothos specimen shows significant chlorosis and structural collapse. The white rabbit anchor is present but the plant is failing to thrive.
plant_audit.P4: Empty soil surface; no signs of life.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Consistently dark and damp across all pots; potential for root rot.
biome_observations.debris: Presence of white particulate matter (likely perlite or mineral salt buildup) on the surface of P3.
biome_observations.anomalies: Introduction of a metallic object (spoon) in the yellow pot (P1) at T-3; introduction of a white cylindrical object (likely a sensor or tube) in the yellow pot (P1) at T-1 and CURRENT.

vision_report.temporal_deltas: Over the 5-day sequence, the biological material has vanished from P1, P2, and P4. P3 has remained static but shows no signs of growth or recovery. The environment has transitioned from a plant-focused setup to one dominated by hardware/sensor interventions.
vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is in a state of critical failure. The lack of visible foliage in P1, P2, and P4 suggests total specimen loss. P3 is the only survivor, but its health is poor, likely due to improper moisture management or lack of light.
vision_report.narrative_description: The audit reveals a catastrophic loss of the botanical collection. Starting from the earliest image, the pots were already sparsely populated. Over the course of the observation period, the plants in P1, P2, and P4 have completely disappeared, leaving only bare soil. P3 (Pothos) is the sole remaining specimen, but it is struggling significantly. The introduction of non-botanical items (spoon, sensor) suggests an attempt to monitor or intervene in the soil conditions, but these have not prevented the systemic collapse of the biome.
